---
title: "Text Analysis GenCom: Generating Code and Analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-07-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

This is a note pad for the data preparation and initial analysis of the General Comments Paper (Network and Diskurs Analysis).

# Transform Documents
The first step is to transform and merge the data. I exported the text data from two MAXQDA files that contain the network coding and all the available texts. MAXQDA only allows exports in Excel, which -- however -- only allows up to ca. 32.000 characters per line. Although the MAXQDA export orinigally stores the text in several lines, the text analysis requires text in one line to generate a comprehensive data set. 
Thus, the transformation needs to transform the text first into CSV and than generate the data set.



## Prepare

```         
install.packages("readxl")
install.packages("ggplot2")

setwd("D:/Research/Web of Human Rights/GenComs/text")

```

## Transforming Excel into CSV
Excel files are stored in one separate folder. CSV files go into a new folder for all texts.

```
library(readxl)

input_dir  <- "all_xlsx"
output_dir <- "all"

files <- list.files(input_dir, pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE)

for (f in files) {
  df <- tryCatch({
    read_excel(f, col_names = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("FEHLER beim Einlesen von ", basename(f), ": ", e$message)
    NULL
  })
  
  if (is.null(df)) next  # Datei überspringen, aber Loop läuft weiter
  
  out_name <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(f)), ".csv")
  out_path <- file.path(output_dir, out_name)
  
  tryCatch({
    write.csv(df, out_path, row.names = FALSE, fileEncoding = "UTF-8")
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("FEHLER beim Schreiben von ", basename(f), ": ", e$message)
  })
}

```

## Batching over all docs
This needs to run over all GenCom Docs now in CSV format.

```         
library(dplyr)

files <- list.files("all", pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
docs_list <- lapply(files, read.csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8")
names(docs_list) <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(files))

collapse_doc <- function(df, text_col = 2) {
  paste(df[[text_col]], collapse = "\n\n")
}

collapsed_all <- sapply(docs_list, collapse_doc)


dataset_text <- data.frame(
  doc_id = names(collapsed_all),
  text   = collapsed_all,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
```

## Exporting Data set just with text

```         
#write text in external file
writeLines(dataset$text, "datatext.txt")

#export dataset
write.csv2(dataset, "dataset_text.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```


## Merge with network data 

```
setwd("D:/Research/Web of Human Rights/GenComs/wd")
library(dplyr)

# Data set 2 with info
data_gc <- read.csv("data_gc.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

## Check
# Values in doc_id without match in data_gc$id 
only_in_text <- setdiff(dataset_text$doc_id, data_gc$id)
only_in_text
length(only_in_text)

# Values in id without match in dataset_text$doc_id
only_in_gc <- setdiff(data_gc$id, dataset_text$doc_id)
only_in_gc
length(only_in_gc)

# Merge
dataset_text <- dataset_text %>% rename(id = doc_id)

dataset_gc <- dataset_text %>%
  inner_join(data_gc, by = "id")

# Check
nrow(merged)
nrow(dataset_text)
nrow(data_gc)

```


## Exporting Data set this version

```         
#export dataset
write.csv2(dataset_gc, "dataset_gc.csv", row.names = FALSE)


View(dataset_gc)                    # View in RStudio
str(dataset_gc$text[87])             # Single text
writeLines(dataset_gc$text[87])      # Read doc

```

## Further code


```



```





## Emerging Problems



# To Do
 (Keep titles) (?)
 
 
# Analysis

## Set-up

```  
setwd("D:/Research/Web of Human Rights/GenComs/wd")
library(ggplot2)

```

## Plotting trend in words


```
dataset_gc |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = words)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess",
  method.args = list(degree = 2, span = 0.75)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number Words",
    title = "Words in General Comments and Recommendations over Time"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```




clustering
sementic distribution
readability
supervised classification


- how is the discursive authority organized, is there a textual dimension to explain the network position

RQ: Does the text explain the network position? 


- specific framing of a new HR interpretation: VaW Gen Com on shifting human rights interpretation, --> look for examples that represent the textual/discursive linkages that you expect
non-technical references --> classification or extraction , name entry recognition task
- What are text level co-variance that make the text (in terms of theory network) central?

- What about GenComs jointly produced by Committees?

+ contructing the Data set
++ Overview Data
- doc_id, text, indegree, outdegree, betweenness, 
- 
++ Dyadic Network Data
- basic doc_id dyadic data

- Identify examples to grasp what these textual representation might actualyl look like.

+ Analysis: supervised extraction, undersupervised extraction








## Including Plots


```

```

# ############################################################################
